shape_decode: cmp=W, vp=W, vpc=_, naive=L — cmplog AND value_profile each independently resolve the gate; naive blocks. A parallel-solvable gate where I2S substitution and VP Hamming/structural-gradient are redundant routes to the SAME target the branch needs.
label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern (cmp W, vp W, naive L). The evidence test is the INDEPENDENT corpus-content verification (G2): confirm BOTH resolving fuzzers' corpora carry / reach the target the gate needs, WITHOUT consulting the resolve pattern itself.

confirmed_frozen.label: parallel_fixed_offset_literal_gate
confirmed_frozen.note: The 6 libpng fixed-offset literal/enum branches confirmed in a prior round under hypothesis parallel_fixed_offset_literal_gate. Their hypotheses are carried forward VERBATIM (superset/monotonic) so they re-validate. Not re-tested, relabeled, or dropped here.

shape_state.round_prior_postmortem: The prior round (recorded in evidence_test.r0.json / the round-2 file) recalibrated thresholds on the SAME two tools (operand_enrichment, joint_necessity) to try to recover these 3 branches. Against the actual arbiter cache they returned rule_not_met (2/2 rules scored, none held). A relaxed-threshold re-test of the same measurement is FORBIDDEN this round. So for each unlabeled branch I propose a GENUINELY DIFFERENT mechanism backed by a tool NOT yet tried on this shape (value_distance_reached / depth_reach), or mark it honestly decidable:false. bloaty_57: its byte_freq_ratio VP arm was unavailable/no_gate_signature and cmplog enrichment alone was insufficient under the prior rule — pivot to value_distance_reached (the Hamming-gradient mechanism the cards actually describe for both arms). sqlite3_14563: its seed-size/token-count joint_necessity arm clipped at the n_W noise floor — pivot to depth_reach (the per-seed ITERATION-DEPTH mechanism the cards actually describe: codeInteger's true branch is driven 32x by a densely-repeated literal body, a property of per-seed reach, NOT seed bulk). libxml2_6785: gate-shape limitation, stays decidable:false.

hypotheses.label: A fixed-offset 1-4 byte VALUE literal/enum gates the branch; both I2S (direct substitution) and VP (Hamming/prefix gradient) drive the gate window toward the target value AND away from the decoy, so BOTH resolving corpora are enriched in the target and depleted in the decoy at the gate offset relative to naive.

params.note: Score the cmplog arm (signed_target_enrich/decoy_enrich, cmplog-vs-naive OE_CACHE) AND the value_profile arm (vp_signed_target_enrich/vp_decoy_enrich, vp-vs-naive OE_VP_CACHE) independently against naive. Per-branch gate offset+target/decoy bytes from the signature: libpng_3864/3951/4085 IHDR color_type offset 0x19 target 0x03 (decoy 0x00/0x04/0x06); libpng_3811/3890 chunk-type offset target 'sPLT' 73 50 4c 54 (decoy other FOURCC); libpng_3818 chunk-validation window.

hypotheses.prediction: Each resolving fuzzer's corpus drives the gate window toward the target literal: EITHER it over-represents the target byte vs naive (signed_target_enrich > 0.5) OR it depletes the decoy byte vs naive (decoy_enrich < -1.0) — the two are complementary corpus-content footprints of the same drive (target enrichment saturates low when naive's baseline target fraction is already high, as at the libpng chunk-type window; decoy depletion is then the cleaner signal). DISCRIMINATING: a structural/length gate returns no_gate_signature; a single-technique-only gate drives the window for ONLY ONE of {cmplog, value_profile}; a non-causal coincidence drives neither. Only the parallel-literal mechanism makes BOTH arms drive the SAME gate window.
hypotheses.g2_independence: operand_enrichment is computed over saved-corpus byte frequencies at the gate offset; it never reads the per-trial resolve/block label that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: The gate is NOT a single fixed-offset literal but a structural/length/accumulation condition (repeated integer-token body, or a multi-comparison validation cascade). Both I2S and VP help by accumulating target tokens / growing the seed, so the resolving seeds are LARGER and carry MORE target tokens than the blocking seeds — there is no single alignable gate byte.

params.note: KEPT VERBATIM from prior round (superset). Aim joint_necessity at the conservative VP-resolving vs naive-blocking arm. Retained so the prior structural footprint stays measured; sqlite3_14563 is ALSO carried by the new depth-iteration hypothesis below, which decision_order tries when this one fails (it did: tag_lift clipped at the n_W noise floor).

hypotheses.prediction: size_lift >= 1.5 (the value_profile-resolving seeds are materially larger than naive-blocking — structural accumulation, not a single-byte flip) AND token_lift >= 1.2 (they carry more integer-literal/operator tokens). DISCRIMINATING: a fixed-offset literal gate would show size_lift ~ 1.0 and be caught by parallel_fixed_offset_literal_gate's enrichment instead; absence of size/token lift means neither technique is accumulating structure.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Seed size + token counts are intrinsic corpus-content properties of the resolving vs blocking seed sets; they do not consult the resolve label.

hypotheses.label: NEW. A fixed-width VALUE operand (a 2-byte enum constant) gates the branch, but the discriminating corpus-content signal is not byte-frequency at one offset — it is HAMMING-DISTANCE CLOSURE: both resolving arms produce seeds whose closest sliding-window value lands at or near the exact gate operand, while naive's seeds stall far from it. Both I2S (lands the operand exactly) and VP (CMP_MAP climbs a Hamming gradient toward it) close the distance; naive cannot. This is the gradient-closure mechanism the cards describe for BOTH arms ('e_machine value closer to 0x3e than any prior input is preserved as a stepping-stone'), measured directly as distance-to-operand rather than as offset byte enrichment.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_prior: Genuinely different from parallel_fixed_offset_literal_gate: that hypothesis used corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio (fraction of corpus carrying the exact target byte at a FIXED offset) and could not score bloaty (VP arm no_gate_signature / cmplog enrichment alone insufficient). This uses seed_set:value_distance_reached (per-arm MIN Hamming distance to the operand over sliding windows) — a different source, compute, and discriminator. It rules out the alternative 'cmplog plants the byte but VP does NOT actually approach the operand' (which byte-freq could not test for the missing-VP-row bloaty case): if VP's seeds do not land closer to 0x003e than naive's, the parallel-gradient claim is refuted.

params.note: EM_X86_64 = 0x003e, the 2-byte little-endian e_machine constant from the switch at elf.cc:782 (signature operand_literal 0x003e, width 2). Slide a 2-byte window over each arm's seeds, take the MIN normalized Hamming distance to 0x003e. Predict BOTH cmplog and value_profile reach distance ~0 (operand landed / climbed-to) while naive stalls. CAVEAT (scope_limit): width=2 is short -> chance-alignment prone; the rule therefore requires a real GAP vs naive (not just winner_closer), so a coincidental near-match in naive cannot pass it.

hypotheses.prediction: Both resolving arms land Hamming-close to 0x003e and materially closer than naive: vp_min_distance and cmp_min_distance are near 0 while naive_min_distance is larger, giving distance_gap > 0 AND distance_closure_ratio high. DISCRIMINATING: (a) if ONLY cmplog closes and VP stays at naive's distance, the parallel claim is refuted (VP is not actually climbing the gradient — falsifies the 'both routes redundant' story). (b) A non-causal coincidence leaves winner and naive at the same distance (distance_gap ~ 0). (c) A structural/moving gate has no fixed 2-byte operand and the tool cannot score it. The 2-byte-operand chance-alignment caveat is countered by requiring the GAP, not bare closeness.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true and distance_gap >= 0.25 and vp_min_distance <= 0.25 and cmp_min_distance <= 0.25
hypotheses.g2_independence: value_distance_reached computes Hamming distance from each arm's saved seeds to the gate operand; it never reads the per-trial resolve/block label. It verifies BOTH resolving corpora independently approached the operand — an independent corpus-content signal, not a re-statement of the resolve pattern.

hypotheses.label: NEW. The gate (codeInteger's true branch) is taken once PER integer literal in a densely-packed operator-joined expression body, so it is an ITERATION-DEPTH gate, not a single literal placement nor mere seed bulk. The resolving seeds drive the branch line MANY MORE times per seed than the blocking seeds (cmplog winner exercises the true side 32x; naive 0x). Both I2S (plants the repeated digit/operator bytes) and VP (CMP_MAP climbs the integer-literal comparisons) produce seeds that REACH the branch repeatedly; naive's short 'SELECT 1;' inputs touch it 0-1x. The discriminator is per-seed-normalized execution depth at the branch line, which a same-size single-flip gate cannot exhibit.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_prior: Genuinely different from parallel_structural_accumulation_gate: that hypothesis used seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count (median seed SIZE + token COUNT of the seed sets) and failed because tag_lift clipped at the small-n_W noise floor while size grew only 2.12x. This uses seed_set:depth_reach (per-seed-normalized EXECUTION count of the branch line via the cov image) — a different source/compute that captures how DEEPLY each seed drives the gate, independent of seed length. It rules out the alternative 'the resolving seeds are merely longer' (longer-but-shallow seeds would show size_lift but NOT depth_lift): if the resolving seeds do not execute codeInteger's true side materially more per seed than naive, the iteration-depth mechanism is refuted.

params.note: codeInteger at sqlite3.c:102982:9 true side. Run winner-resolving (cmplog and value_profile) vs naive-blocking seeds through libafl-sqlite3-cov; read the per-seed-normalized true-side execution count at line 102982. sqlite3 is on-disk-corpus + has a cov image. Predict winner_depth >> loser_depth (true side hit ~32x per winning seed vs ~0 for naive's 'SELECT 1;' losers).

hypotheses.prediction: depth_lift >= 2.0 (resolving seeds drive codeInteger's true branch materially more times per seed than naive) AND winner_deeper == true. DISCRIMINATING: (a) a single fixed-offset literal gate (h1) is taken at most once per seed -> depth_lift ~ 1.0; only a repeated-token iteration body lifts depth. (b) 'resolving seeds are merely longer but execute the branch the same per-seed' -> depth_lift ~ 1.0, refuting the iteration-depth mechanism (and correctly distinguishing it from raw seed bulk). (c) if neither resolving arm reaches the branch deeper than naive, the mechanism is refuted and the branch falls to fallback.
hypotheses.g2_independence: depth_reach reads the cov-image per-seed execution count at the branch line for the resolving vs blocking seed sets; it never consults the per-trial resolve/block label that defined the shape. Confirming the resolving corpora drive the branch deeper per seed is an independent corpus-reach signal.

decidable_false.reason: The gate is `CUR == 0` at libxml2's MOVING parse cursor (the NUL byte must land at a position determined by parse state after optional 4-byte encoding detection), so there is NO FIXED input offset for any built tool to place. (1) operand_enrichment (byte_freq_ratio) returns no_gate_signature for both arms (no fixed offset). (2) value_distance_reached scope_limits flag width<=2 operands as chance-alignment-prone (here width=1, a single NUL 0x00) AND it needs a fixed operand window to slide against — a 1-byte all-zeros 'operand' matches any low/zero byte anywhere, so a Hamming-distance gap vs naive is uninterpretable (naive seeds contain incidental zero bytes too). (3) depth_reach measures iteration depth, but CUR==0 is a single end-of-buffer test taken once, not an iteration gate. No built measurement can discriminate the parallel-NUL mechanism from coincidence for a moving 1-byte all-zero gate. This is a gate-SHAPE limitation (true even if libxml2 corpus were local), not a corpus-availability gap. Honest decidable:false per G3 — no measurement was invented to force a label.

data_realities_respected.corpus_availability: All three new/kept measurements (operand_enrichment, joint_necessity, value_distance_reached, depth_reach) need on-disk corpus / resolving+blocking seeds. Per data_realities on-disk corpora exist for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 (s4) and lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty (sB). sqlite3 (depth_reach H4 + joint_necessity H2) is local to s4; libpng (operand_enrichment H1) and bloaty (value_distance_reached H3) are owned by sB, which arbitrates them on its own corpus. Per the cross-server rule, NO branch is marked decidable:false merely because its corpus is off this server — H3/bloaty is scored by the owning server.
data_realities_respected.i2s_untagged: No descriptor relies on a tagged 'I2S event' in seed_lineage. All four are corpus-content / corpus-reach measurements (byte enrichment, seed size+token, Hamming distance-to-operand, per-seed execution depth) — the only feasible way to observe I2S and VP per data_realities.
data_realities_respected.value_profile_is_feedback_not_mutator: VP is measured only via what its corpus reaches: byte enrichment (H1), seed size/tokens (H2), Hamming-distance closure to the operand (H3), and branch-line execution depth (H4) — never via a lineage 'gradient event' (which does not exist).
data_realities_respected.byte_alignment_only_for_fixed_gates: byte_freq_ratio (H1) applies ONLY to fixed-offset value gates (the 6 libpng enum/FOURCC branches). The 2-byte bloaty enum is also fixed-width but pivoted to value_distance_reached (H3) because its VP byte-freq arm was unavailable and Hamming-closure is the mechanism its cards actually describe. The structural sqlite3 branch uses seed_set tools (struct_size+token / depth_reach), never byte alignment. The moving-cursor libxml2 branch is decidable:false.

notes.priors_kept: Both prior KEEP hypotheses retained VERBATIM (rule unchanged): parallel_fixed_offset_literal_gate (operand_enrichment) re-validates the 6 frozen libpng branches, and parallel_structural_accumulation_gate (joint_necessity) is carried forward over sqlite3_14563. Superset/monotonic invariant satisfied — no confirmed branch can regress.
notes.new_mechanisms: Two NEW hypotheses for the unlabeled branches, each a genuinely different mechanism on a tool NOT tried before on this shape (no relaxed-threshold re-test of the failed prior rules): (H3) parallel_hamming_gradient_value_closure via value_distance_reached for bloaty_57 — recasts the 2-byte EM_X86_64 enum as Hamming-distance closure (the gradient mechanism both cards describe), discriminating because it requires BOTH arms to land closer to 0x003e than naive with a real gap (countering the width=2 chance-alignment caveat). (H4) parallel_literal_iteration_depth_gate via depth_reach for sqlite3_14563 — recasts codeInteger's true branch as a per-seed iteration-depth gate (true side hit ~32x per winning seed), discriminating because it requires per-seed execution depth (not seed bulk) to lift, distinguishing a repeated-token body from a merely-longer seed.
notes.decidable_false: libxml2_6785 (both halves) — gate-SHAPE limitation: moving parse cursor + single all-zero 1-byte operand means no built tool (fixed-offset enrichment, Hamming-window distance, or iteration depth) can discriminate the parallel-NUL mechanism from coincidence. Honest inconclusive per G3, not corpus-availability.
notes.bloaty_not_decidable_false: bloaty_57 is NOT decidable:false despite its prior byte-freq VP-arm gap: value_distance_reached is a different, applicable measurement for its fixed 2-byte operand, and bloaty's corpus is owned by sB (cross-server rule — design the test, let the owner arbitrate). It is left to H3, scored by sB.
notes.discrimination_summary: decision_order tries H1 (fixed-offset byte enrichment) -> H3 (Hamming-distance closure) -> H2 (seed size/token) -> H4 (per-seed iteration depth). The four are mutually discriminable: H1 needs a fixed offset with target enrichment/decoy depletion and size_lift~1.0; H3 needs a fixed-width operand both arms close on with a gap; H2 needs larger seeds with more tokens; H4 needs deeper per-seed execution independent of size. A branch validates under the FIRST rule that holds; membership lists are documentation, the arbiter applies all rules per branch in order.
